lede: A look at December 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Martin Schulz and Martyn Lloyd-Jones, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: American Airlines Flight 965
events.desc: American Airlines Flight 965, a Boeing 757, crashes into a mountain 50 km north of Cali, Colombia, killing 159 of the 163 people on board.

events.title: Pope John Paul II
events.desc: Pope John Paul II announces the creation of World Youth Day.

events.title: Murder of Jonelle Matthews
events.desc: Disappearance of Jonelle Matthews from Greeley, Colorado. Her remains were discovered on July 23, 2019, located about 24 km (15 mi) southeast of Jonelle's home. The cause of death "was a gunshot wound to the head."

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: The National Liberation Front of South Vietnam, popularly known as the Viet Cong, is formally established in Tân Lập village, present day Tây Ninh province.

events.title: Boeing 707
events.desc: The initial production version of the Boeing 707 makes its first flight.

events.title: Experimental Breeder Reactor I
events.desc: The EBR-1 in Arco, Idaho becomes the first nuclear power plant to generate electricity. The electricity powered four light bulbs.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: First battle of the American Volunteer Group, better known as the "Flying Tigers", in Kunming, China.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler is released from Landsberg Prison.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The last Australian troops are evacuated from Gallipoli.

events.title: South Carolina
events.desc: South Carolina becomes the first state to attempt to secede from the United States with the South Carolina Declaration of Secession.

births.name: Kazimierz Marcinkiewicz
births.line: Polish physicist and politician, 12th Prime Minister of Poland

births.name: Otto Graf Lambsdorff
births.line: German lawyer and politician, German Federal Minister of Economics

deaths.text: Carl Sagan, American astronomer, astrophysicist, and cosmologist
Dmitry Ustinov, Minister of Defence of the Soviet Union
John Steinbeck, American novelist and short story writer, Nobel Prize laureate
Kangxi Emperor, emperor of the Qing Dynasty

observances.text: Abolition of Slavery Day, also known as Fête des Cafres (Réunion, French Guiana)
